title: The Sun On The Bookcase
content:
(Student's Love-song)



    Once more the cauldron of the sun
    Smears the bookcase with winy red,
    And here my page is, and there my bed,
    And the apple-tree shadows travel along.
    Soon their intangible track will be run,
        And dusk grow strong
        And they be fled.

    Yes: now the boiling ball is gone,
    And I have wasted another day . . .
    But wasted WASTED, do I say?
    Is it a waste to have imaged one
    Beyond the hills there, who, anon,
        My great deeds done
        Will be mine alway?
meaning:
Thomas Hardy's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "The Sun On The Bookcase"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
